questions.question_text: According to Faraday's law for a coil having N turns, the total induced emf is given by
questions.question_html: According to Faraday's law for a coil having N turns, the total induced emf is given by
questions.explanation: The induced electromotive force in any closed circuit is equal to the negative of the time rate of change of the magnetic flux enclosed by the circuit. Induced emf for a coil, {tex}e=-\frac{d\phi_B}{dt}{/tex} The flux of all the turns will be added up. So the induced emf for a coil having N turns will be {tex}e=-N\frac{d\phi_B}{dt}{/tex}

questions.explanation: In this case we would be obtaining electrical energy continuously without doing any work.

questions.question_html: A rectangular conductor PQRS in which the conductor PQ is free to move is kept in a magnetic field B, which is perpendicular to the PQRS which lies in a single plane. If the conductor PQ of length l is pushed by a force F then the power supplied is (assuming a circuit ressistance r)
questions.explanation: P = Fv {tex}F = iBl{/tex} {tex}i = {e \over r} = {{Bvl} \over r}{/tex} {tex}F = iBl = {{{B^2}{l^2}v} \over r}{/tex} P = {tex}{{{B^2}{l^2}v^2} \over r}{/tex}

options.text: currents which circulate in conductors like swirling eddies in a stream.
options.html: currents which circulate in conductors like swirling eddies in a stream.

options.text: induced solar currents that circulate through out a river and are swirling
options.html: induced solar currents that circulate through out a river and are swirling

questions.correct_option_text: currents which circulate in conductors like swirling eddies in a stream.
questions.explanation: Eddy currents (also called Foucault currents) are loops of electrical current induced within conductors by a changing magnetic field in the conductor, due to Faraday's law of induction. Eddy currents flow in closed loops within conductors, in planes perpendicular to the magnetic field. By lenz's law an eddy current creates a magnetic field that opposes a change in the magnetic field that created it, and thus eddy currents react back on the source of the magnetic field.
